check.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "$apps=@('excel.exe','groove.exe','lync.exe','msaccess.exe','mspub.exe','onenote.exe','outlook.exe','powerpnt.exe','visio.exe','winproj.exe','winword.exe'); $roots=@($env:ProgramFiles,${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}) | Where-Object { $_ }; $installed=$apps | Where-Object { $app=$_; $roots | Where-Object { Test-Path (Join-Path $_ ('Microsoft Office\root\Office16\' + $app)) -or Test-Path (Join-Path $_ ('Microsoft Office\Office16\' + $app)) } }; $key='HKLM:\SOFTWARE\MICROSOFT\INTERNET EXPLORER\MAIN\FEATURECONTROL\FEATURE_RESTRICT_ACTIVEXINSTALL'; $bad=$installed | Where-Object { $props=Get-ItemProperty -Path $key -Name $_ -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $props.PSObject.Properties[$_].Value -ne 1 }; if (($installed | Measure-Object).Count -gt 0 -and -not $bad) { 'Compliant' }"

candidate_check.description: ActiveX installation restriction must be enabled in all Office programs.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Restrict ActiveX Install is set to "Enabled" and the check box is selected for every installed Office program.

Use the Windows Registry Editor to navigate to the following key:

HKLM\software\microsoft\internet explorer\main\featurecontrol\feature_restrict_activexinstall

If the value for all installed programs is REG_DWORD = 1, this is not a finding.
fix_text_excerpt: Set the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Microsoft Office 2016 (Machine) >> Security Settings >> IE Security >> Restrict ActiveX Install to "Enabled" and select the check boxes for  all installed Office programs.
title: ActiveX installation restriction must be enabled in all Office programs.
